mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 55%, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of elevated inflation signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of high-inflation periods with rising industrial production, or the conditional predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic expansion and higher aggregate income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or payroll growth shows no correlation with 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 5.0 percent imposes severe financing costs on leveraged corporate balance sheets, suppressing investment and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >60% of periods where the federal funds rate surpasses 5.0 percent, or the threshold effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates weak household demand despite macro growth, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.0% quarterly while RSAFS declines
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns with >55% accuracy, or the interaction effect disappears in multivariate regression controls.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signal demand destruction rather than supply optimization, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes during tightening periods yield positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the regime-specific signal loses predictive power.
